item.title: BPF Map Poisoning: Attacking the EDR from the Inside
item.description: Most evasion techniques try to avoid the EDR. This one walks straight into it and writes to its memory.

item.title: Trend Micro Deep Security Agent Research: Forcing bmhook/tmhook Reloads to Open a Protection Bypass Window
item.description: Security research on a local event-storm condition that makes Trend Micro Deep Security Agent unload and reload bmhook/tmhook, creating a repeatable protection bypass window.

item.title: Breaking eBPF Security: How Kernel Rootkits Blind Observability Tools
item.description: Deep technical analysis of bypassing eBPF-based security solutions through kernel-level hooks targeting BPF iterators, ringbuffers, and perf events

item.title: Ioctl Secrets Writeup
item.description: Solving an easy reversing challenge from rootkit researchers.

item.title: Evading Elastic Security: Linux Rootkit Detection Bypass
item.description: Bypassing YARA rules and behavioral detection through symbol randomization, module fragmentation, XOR encoding, and ICMP reverse shell staging

item.title: Red Team Tactics: Evading EDR on Linux with io_uring
item.description: Learn how to bypass modern defenses with io_uring

item.title: Bypassing LD_PRELOAD Rootkits Is Easy
item.description: Learn how to bypass LD_PRELOAD rootkits.

item.title: Detecting rootkits based on ftrace hooking.
item.description: Learn how to detect rootkit based on ftrace hooking.

item.title: How detect a LD_PRELOAD rootkit and hide from ldd & /proc
item.description: Learn how to detect and hide a LD_PRELOAD rootkit from ldd, /proc/pid/maps, etc.

item.title: A simple way to detect and remove LKM rootkit KoviD (Outdated)
item.description: Learn a simple way on how to detect and remove Kovid rootkit.

item.title: Linux Threat Hunting Persistence
item.description: Learn about persistence in Linux and how to hunt
